about: With over three years of work experience in machine learning, quantum computing, and education, I am passionate about creating and delivering cutting-edge courses that empower learners to master the skills and concepts of the future. My mission is to inspire and equip students, professionals, and educators with the tools and knowledge they need to succeed in the rapidly evolving fields of AI and quantum software engineering. I bring diverse perspectives and experiences to the team, having worked with leading organizations such as Google Quantum AI, IBM Quantum, Amazon Web Services, and Harvard University. Until November 2022, I was a course developer and lead instructor at The Coding School, where I designed and taught machine learning and quantum computing courses for various audiences and partners. I created 12 machine learning lectures and ten problem sets using Pandas, PyTorch, Sci-Kit Learn, and AWS SageMaker for the AWS Train AI professional development course. I also developed and taught lessons, labs, and exercises in intensive courses on computer science and quantum software engineering for the Early Quantum Career Immersion (EQCI) program, in partnership with Google Quantum AI, IBM Quantum, Qunnect, BP, and the Unitary Fund. In both roles, I used my skills in leadership, research, and data analysis to deliver engaging and rigorous content that challenged and motivated the learners.… more

Recent post: "Excited to join Ramp's engineering team." — Starting a new position at Ramp. 87 reactions, 6 comments.

From Lana Martin, Ph.D., Senior Program Analyst at the State Bar of California’s Office of Access & Inclusion (August 26, 2022, Lana managed Phil directly):

"Phil is an incredibly talented and dedicated instructor and content developer who clearly knows what it takes to help students and his teammates succeed. I have had the pleasure of supervising Phil while he teaches, and while our team plans, designs, produces, and assesses instructional deliverables for in-person and virtual STEM workshops and courses. I have mentored teachers for 10 years, and Phil stands out as an exceptionally patient and effective instructor, tutor, and mentor. Phil makes it fun for students to learn difficult subjects – like quantum computing – in technical areas that are often ambiguous because they are actively being developed. I am particularly impressed by Phil’s skill with teaching in the virtual format. He really nails content pacing even for courses with logistically challenging synchronous and asynchronous components. Phil has a wonderful set of engagement strategies that I have seen build rapport and comradery among students to a truly inspiring level. I am especially impressed by Phil’s ability to research technical concepts and professional standards in areas new to our team, then quickly map an effective plan for our organization to meet or exceed academic and industry partners’ expectations. While developing new learning paths and materials for high school, undergraduate, and post-graduate learners, he shows a particularly keen eye for balancing learning objectives and competencies between technical and leadership components. Our students came for the Python and Qiskit, but they absolutely loved staying for the team building environment that Phil facilitated. In addition to his amazing work ethic, Phil’s ability to motivate colleagues and students – no matter how overwhelming the task at hand – makes him a phenomenal asset to any team, be they instructors, content developers, researchers, consultants, or engineers!… more
